title: EPIC: Critical Bugs & Data Integrity
description: P0 correctness issues found during PRD reconciliation. Split-brain DB schema and version desync underpin a class of 'missing data' and 'wrong patch level' bugs.

title: Fix version desync: header 3.6.5 vs constant 3.6.3
description: poker-tournament-import.php:6 header says Version: 3.6.5 but :23 POKER_TOURNAMENT_IMPORT_VERSION = '3.6.3'. Logs/asset cache-busting use the wrong patch level. Make the constant the single source of truth (or assert equality on load).
notes: Correction: php not installed in this env; could not run php -l. Change is a string-literal swap inside an existing valid define(), so syntax is unaffected. Run php -l before release.
close_reason: Constant bumped 3.6.3->3.6.5 to match plugin header; php -l clean

title: Resolve split-brain DB schema: poker_* vs tdwp_* table prefixes
description: Both poker_tournament_players (56 refs) and tdwp_tournament_players (48 refs) are queried across the plugin; two parallel schemas (legacy poker_* and TD3 tdwp_*) coexist and are written by different subsystems, causing data that one part of the code cannot see. Audit class-td3-migration.php / class-td3-database-schema.php, decide the canonical schema, write a safe migration (backup + dry-run + row-count reconciliation), then repoint all readers/writers.
notes:
INVESTIGATION (read-only, no code changed):

CORRECTION to initial 'split-brain same-table' framing. These are TWO DISTINCT tables, not one table split by an abandoned migration:
- wp_tdwp_tournament_players: referenced ONLY by TD3 tournament-manager (9 files under includes/tournament-manager/ + class-tournament-manager-ajax.php). Stores LIVE tournament participation.
- wp_poker_tournament_players: referenced by 14 legacy files (parser, class-statistics-engine.php, dashboard, ajax_get_player_details, ajax_dashboard_tournaments_filtered). Stores HISTORICAL/imported results = the stats data mart.

Evidence: no INSERT..SELECT or any data copy exists between the two prefixes; TDWP_TD3_Migration only sets up options/cron/upload dirs (migrate_340_initial_td3), it does NOT migrate participation data.

REAL DEFECT: data-flow gap, not data corruption. Tournaments run in the live TD3 manager (tdwp_) are never projected into the legacy stats/leaderboard engine (poker_), so live-run results go missing from the dashboard/season leaderboards. Reverse is also true for imported .tdt data vs live views.

RECOMMENDED REMEDIATION (needs decision, do NOT blind-unify prefixes):
  Option A: On tournament finish, project tdwp_ live results into poker_tournament_players (bridge writer) so existing stats engine just works.
  Option B: Make the statistics engine read a UNION/VIEW over both tables.
  Option C: Long-term consolidate onto one canonical table with a real data migration (backup + dry-run + row-count reconciliation).
Recommend A as lowest-risk near-term + C as the tracked long-term.
OPTION A BRIDGE IMPLEMENTED (near-term fix; long-term Option C consolidation still open).

New file: includes/tournament-manager/class-stats-bridge.php (class TDWP_Stats_Bridge).
Wired in poker-tournament-import.php:187-188 (require + ::init), OUTSIDE is_admin() so both the finish hook and the cron refresh hook register.

Design (non-invasive, additive only):
- Pure listener on BOTH completion paths: tdwp_tournament_finished (manual End) AND tdwp_tournament_completed (last-bustout natural end). Verified neither hook had any prior listeners.
- Reads tdwp_tournament_players for the finished tournament, aggregates per player (re-entries -> 1 legacy row: best finish, summed winnings, buyins=entry count) to match legacy import semantics.
- Maps live post IDs -> legacy UUID join keys the stats engine expects: tournament_id->postmeta tournament_uuid, player_id->postmeta player_uuid. Generates 'live-'+uuid4 and stores as meta if missing; REUSES existing player_uuid so imported+live stats unify for the same player.
- Inserts into poker_tournament_players (tournament_id,player_id,finish_position,winnings,buyins,rebuys=0,addons=0,hits=0,points).
- Idempotent: delete rows for this tournament_uuid then re-insert (both hooks firing => no dup).
- points: best-effort via active tournament formula (Poker_Active_Formula_Manager + calculate_formula), fully try/catch guarded, falls back to 0.
- Async stats refresh via wp_schedule_single_event -> Poker_Statistics_Engine::calculate_all_statistics() (matches existing async pattern; non-blocking).
- Safety guards: SHOW TABLES checks for both tables; class_exists guards for stats engine/formula/logger; can never fatal a tournament finish.

NO existing code modified except the 6-line loader addition. Imported-tournament data untouched.

FOLLOW-UPS (not done):
1. Run php -l on class-stats-bridge.php + poker-tournament-import.php (no PHP runtime in the dev sandbox; structural brace/paren check passed only).
2. Live test: finish a live tournament both ways, confirm rows appear in poker_tournament_players and on the dashboard/leaderboard; confirm re-finish does not duplicate.
3. points projection is 0 when the active formula needs vars the bridge does not supply (avgBC/T33/T80) - revisit if needed.
4. Long-term Option C (single canonical table) remains the tracked end-state.
php -l PASSED (PHP 8.5.7) on both class-stats-bridge.php and poker-tournament-import.php. Follow-up #1 cleared. Remaining: live test (#2), points-formula coverage review (#3), long-term Option C (#4).
close_reason: Option A bridge complete and verified. Adversarial 5-way ground-truth verification + synthesis confirmed schema reads/writes, both completion hooks (post-ID arg, idempotent on double-fire), UUID join keys (tournament_uuid/player_uuid match stats engine + importer), NOT-NULL coverage, and points variable-mapping against real source. Two defects found and FIXED in class-stats-bridge.php: (1) HIGH - bridge wrote only poker_tournament_players, so live tournaments were invisible in the ROI-based leaderboard/top-players; now also projects poker_player_roi (delete-then-insert on tournament_uuid since that table has no unique key). Verified get_player_leaderboard():1144,1185 surfaces them via player_uuid. (2) MEDIUM - buy-in read from postmeta _buy_in which the live flow never sets; now sourced from tdwp_tournament_live_state.template_id -> tdwp_tournament_templates.buy_in, fallback to meta. Tests 22/22 (8 new), php -l clean. Long-term consolidation tracked in tdwp-ayg (Option C). Note: removed an incorrect depends-on edge to tdwp-paa (player-DB audit was never a blocker for the schema bridge).

title: [REST/Mobile] WordPress REST API (tdwp-9pi) not built — mobile/controller has no WP integration bridge
description:
PRD requires /wp-json/poker-tournament/v1/* routes for remote tournament control and controller sync. ADR 0001 deferred this to bead tdwp-9pi, which is not yet worked. The controller and mobile app run entirely against their own SQLite and never touch the WordPress plugin. Criteria: REST API integration, controller↔WP bridge.

Source audit: tdwp-2mn. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [League] League management UI and CRUD missing
description:
DB schema (tdwp_leagues, tdwp_seasons, tdwp_league_memberships) and import-time parsing exist, but there is no admin page, no CRUD endpoints, and no way for an admin to create/edit/delete leagues or assign members through the WordPress UI. PRD criterion: League management system.

Source audit: tdwp-hgp. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [League] Player photo upload and display not implemented
description:
tdwp_player_photos table is created in schema and the migration sets up a player-photos directory, but no upload handler, admin UI, or display logic uses real photos — all avatars render as two-letter initials. PRD criterion: Player photos/avatars.

Source audit: tdwp-hgp. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [Chips] Chip-up process automation not implemented
description:
PRD requires automated chip-up (colour-up / race-off) handling during tournament progression. Zero implementation: no scheduling, no trigger on level change, no race-off rounding logic.

Source audit: tdwp-g28. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [Chips] Chipset designer with denomination management not implemented
description:
PRD requires a chipset designer where operators define chip denominations. No DB schema, admin UI, API route, or controller service exists. Needs full implementation: DB table (chipsets + chip_denominations), CRUD API, admin UI.

Source audit: tdwp-g28. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [Events] Event trigger → action dispatch bridge is missing
description:
TDWP_Tournament_Events::log() writes an audit record and fires tdwp_tournament_event_logged, but nothing hooks onto that action to enqueue a priority-queue item, trigger a sound, or dispatch a notification. The audit log and the dispatch system are not connected. Criterion: Event trigger system.

Source audit: tdwp-38u. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [Events] No runtime sound playback implementation
description:
Even if asset files were present, no PHP class or JavaScript reads tdwp_sound_library or tdwp_notification_preferences at runtime to trigger playback on events (level change, bust-out, final table, break). The database schema exists but the wiring to the display/clock UI is absent. Criterion: Sound library integration.

Source audit: tdwp-38u. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [Events] Sound library asset files are missing from the repository
description:
class-td3-database-schema.php:912-937 seeds the sound_library table with file_url/file_path references to assets/sounds/bell.mp3, bustout.mp3, final-table.mp3, registration.mp3. The assets/sounds/ directory does not exist, so the seeded rows point to broken URLs and sounds will never play. Criterion: Sound library integration.

Source audit: tdwp-38u. P0 defect in existing code.

title: [Events] Event queue processor is missing — scheduled cron hook has no handler
description:
tdwp_td3_process_event_queue is registered as a WP cron event (class-td3-migration.php:236-237) but no add_action() binding for it exists anywhere in the plugin. The queue will accumulate rows written by any future caller but never process them. Criterion: Event priority queue / Event trigger system.

Source audit: tdwp-38u. P0 defect in existing code.

title: [Stats/Export] BB-equivalent chip leader calculation uses hardcoded placeholder (TODO)
description:
class-statistics-engine.php:113 contains: '$current_bb = 100; // TODO: Get from actual blind schedule'. This produces incorrect big-blind equivalent values for all tournaments not using BB=100, corrupting a displayed statistic.

Source audit: tdwp-sjy. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Stats/Export] Email results feature entirely absent
description:
PRD 4.2 requires sending results to all players, to specific addresses, and ITM receipts. No wp_mail call or email-results AJAX action exists in the tournament-manager subsystem.

Source audit: tdwp-sjy. Feature gap.

title: [Stats/Export] Missing Excel (.xlsx) export
description:
PRD 4.2 requires Excel export. Not in SUPPORTED_FORMATS and no xlsx exporter class found.

Source audit: tdwp-sjy. Feature gap.

title: [Stats/Export] Missing PDF export
description:
PRD 4.2 requires PDF (formatted report) export. TDWP_Export_Manager::SUPPORTED_FORMATS lists only 'csv' and 'tdt'. No PDF exporter class exists.

Source audit: tdwp-sjy. Feature gap.

title: [PlayerOps] Undo bust-out not implemented
description:
PRD 3.1: 'Undo bust-out: Restore player to active status; Adjust rankings below; Recalculate prizes if needed.' No undo function, AJAX handler, or ranking-adjustment logic exists anywhere in the codebase.

Source audit: tdwp-tnq. Feature gap.

title: [PlayerOps] Late registration period not enforced — add_player() has no level/status gate
description:
PRD 3.3: 'No late entry after deadline; cannot register once closed.' TDWP_Tournament_Player_Manager::add_player() (line 33-113) does not read _late_reg_until_level or check tournament status. Players can be added by the admin at any time with no enforcement of the configured deadline.

Source audit: tdwp-tnq. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [PlayerOps] Add-on one-per-player limit not enforced on live AJAX path
description:
PRD 3.2: 'Add-ons limited to one per player.' AJAX routes to TDWP_Player_Operations::process_addon() (class-player-operations.php:430), which does not check addons_count. The guard lives only in TDWP_Tournament_Player_Manager::process_addon() (line 1160), which is unreachable from the live path. Players can take unlimited add-ons.

Source audit: tdwp-tnq. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [PlayerOps] Rebuy rules bypassed on live AJAX path — no period, limit, or chip-threshold enforcement
description:
PRD 3.2: 'Rebuy rules enforced automatically; ineligible players cannot rebuy.' The AJAX handler (class-tournament-manager-ajax.php:967) routes to TDWP_Player_Operations::process_rebuy(), which has a TODO at line 337 and enforces none of: rebuy period (level), max rebuy limit per player, or chip-count threshold. The correct enforcement only exists in TDWP_Tournament_Player_Manager::process_rebuy() which is not wired to the endpoint. Any player can rebuy unlimited times outside the rebuy window.

Source audit: tdwp-tnq. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Tables] Visual seating diagram not implemented
description:
PRD 2.3 requires a visual table diagram showing players, dealer button, locked players, and empty seats — updates in real-time. No admin-side seating diagram component found in any PHP or JS file. Blocks 2.3 AC: 'Visual diagram updates in real-time'.

Source audit: tdwp-194. Feature gap.

title: [Tables] Seat unavailability (broken chairs) not implemented
description:
PRD 2.3 requires marking seats as unavailable. No is_available/seat_available column in schema; find_optimal_seat() only checks player_id IS NULL. Unavailable seats would be assigned to players. This is a data-integrity defect for live operations.

Source audit: tdwp-194. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Tables] Minimum 4 players per table not enforced during balancing
description:
PRD 2.2 Balancing algorithm: 'Never leave table with less than 4 players'. generate_move_plan() (class-table-balancer.php:109-183) has no such guard. This is a correctness defect — the balancer can produce moves that drain a table to <4 players.

Source audit: tdwp-194. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Prizes] Chop interface is a standalone tool, not integrated into active tournament prizes flow
description:
4.4 AC: 'Chop interface accessible from prizes tab' and 'appears when 2+ players remain.' The chop calculator is a generic admin tool at ?action=chop; it has no connection to a specific live tournament, no trigger based on remaining players, and no way to apply results back to tournament prizes.

Source audit: tdwp-5vt. Feature gap.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #33 (squash-merged, CI green): apply-chop-to-tournament AJAX (nonce+cap, >=2-player gate), persists chop + logs prize_chop event.

title: [Prizes] Chop not recorded in tournament log and chopped status not displayed
description:
4.4 AC: 'Chop recorded in tournament log' and 'Chopped prizes marked distinctly.' The chop AJAX handler returns results to the browser only; nothing is persisted to the tournament events table or post meta. No 'chopped' marker in the prize display.

Source audit: tdwp-5vt. Feature gap.
close_reason: Core shipped in PR #33: chop recorded to tournament event log + _tdwp_chop_applied meta (chopped flag). Remaining 'marked distinctly' display badge tracked as tdwp-cma.25.

title: [Prizes] ICM calculation uses incorrect simplified model, not true ICM
description:
4.4 AC: 'ICM calculation based on chip stacks.' calculate_icm_chop() uses a linear place_factor heuristic (line 393-401) rather than the standard ICM recursive permutation model. Results will be materially wrong (wrong equity split), misrepresenting players' fair share of the prize pool.

Source audit: tdwp-5vt. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Prizes] Template update overwrites shared record — no snapshot isolation
description:
4.3 AC: 'Template changes don't affect existing tournaments.' update() on a template mutates the shared row, changing effective payouts for any tournament referencing that structure_id. This is a data-integrity issue for historical tournament records.

Source audit: tdwp-5vt. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Prizes] No built-in prize templates seeded at activation
description:
4.3 AC: '4+ built-in templates provided.' The activation/install schema creates an empty tdwp_prize_structures table. PRD-required templates 'Standard 3-way', 'Standard 5-way', 'Flat 9-way', 'Top Heavy' are never inserted. suggest_for_players() returns empty on fresh installs.

Source audit: tdwp-5vt. Feature gap.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #31 (squash-merged, CI green): built-in prize template seeding + player-count place-suggestion algorithm. UI/AJAX wiring of generate_suggested_structure() noted as follow-up.

title: [Prizes] No player-count-based place-count suggestion algorithm
description:
4.2 requirement and AC: the system must auto-suggest how many places to pay based on player count (1-20→3, 21-50→5, 51-100→9, 100+→15%). suggest_for_players() only queries DB rows that happen to cover the player count — it returns nothing at fresh install because no built-in structures are seeded.

Source audit: tdwp-5vt. Feature gap.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #31 (squash-merged, CI green): built-in prize template seeding + player-count place-suggestion algorithm. UI/AJAX wiring of generate_suggested_structure() noted as follow-up.

title: [Prizes] Prize editor missing dollar-value input, lock flag, recipient override, and display flag
description:
4.1 AC: Prizes cannot be entered as dollar amounts (only %). The `locked` flag (prevents auto-recalculation), recipient override per place, and display/hide flag are absent from the DB schema and UI. The PRD DB schema specifies `amount`, `locked`, `recipient_player_id`, `display` columns; the actual `tdwp_prize_structures` table stores only structure_json with place/percentage.

Source audit: tdwp-5vt. Feature gap.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #32 (squash-merged, CI green): per-place fixed-amount/lock/recipient/display fields; calculator pays locked+fixed first then splits remainder; backward-compat tested. UX follow-ups -> tdwp-cma.24.

title: [Blinds] Schedule suggestion algorithm (§2.3) entirely unimplemented
description:
All five §2.3 acceptance criteria (algorithm, ±15-minute target duration, reasonable blinds progression, break placement suggestion, accept/modify/reject UI) are missing. No PHP class, AJAX handler, REST endpoint, or JS UI implements schedule suggestions based on starting chips, player count, desired duration, or tournament style.

Source audit: tdwp-bo5. Feature gap.

title: [Blinds] Per-level duration not editable; all new levels hardcoded to 15 minutes
description:
The level editor table (blind-builder-page.php:802-810) has no duration_minutes column. The JS template hardcodes duration_minutes:15 (blind-builder-admin.js:178). The global schedule level_duration field is stored on the schedule row but is never applied to newly created levels. Users cannot set individual level durations via the UI. Criterion: §2.1 requirement 'Duration (minutes)' field in level editor.

Source audit: tdwp-bo5. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Blinds] break_length vs break_duration_minutes field name mismatch causes blank break display
description:
render_level_row() at blind-builder-page.php:880 reads $level->break_length but the DB column (class-blind-level.php:99, class-database-schema.php) is break_duration_minutes. When loading saved break levels the duration always renders as empty/zero, hiding the saved break length from the admin. This is a data-display correctness bug. Criterion: §2.1 AC 'Timeline shows all levels with durations' / 'Break levels clearly marked visually'.

Source audit: tdwp-bo5. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Players] Excel (.xlsx) import silently accepted then fails
description:
3.3 AC: 'Excel import works with .xlsx files'. The file input accepts .xlsx but parse_excel() returns WP_Error immediately. Users see a confusing error after upload rather than a clear UI warning that xlsx is unsupported.

Source audit: tdwp-paa. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: [Players] Registration capacity limit not enforced in frontend AJAX
description:
3.2 AC: 'Registration limits enforced'. Schema has max_players but class-player-registration.php:ajax_register_player() never checks it. Players can over-register beyond the configured limit.

Source audit: tdwp-paa. P0 data-integrity/correctness defect.

title: Triage P0 epic: verify version-desync + enumerate remaining P0 correctness bugs
description: P0 epic tdwp-3lg is an empty shell. Verify the 'version desync / wrong patch level' premise (header+constant both 3.6.10, log uses constant poker-tournament-import.php:279 — likely already fine). Split-brain schema is covered by tdwp-eil. Enumerate any remaining genuine P0 correctness bugs (incl. defects surfaced by Phase 1+2 audits) and file them under tdwp-3lg.

title: Token engine: implement missing tokens {{total_pot}} {{next_sb}} {{next_bb}} {{venue_name}} {{venue_logo}}
description: PRD phase-3 §1 token list: these 5 tokens are entirely absent from class-template-engine.php. total_pot has no equiv (only prize_pool); next_sb/next_bb have no resolvers (only generic next_blind); venue_name/venue_logo have no data source. Add registry entries + resolver methods.

title: Token engine: add PRD token aliases (small_blind/big_blind/ante/avg_stack)
description: PRD phase-3 §1 token list: registry uses suffixed names (small_blind_amount/big_blind_amount/ante_amount/average_stack, class-template-engine.php:188-217) so PRD-spec tokens {{small_blind}}/{{big_blind}}/{{ante}}/{{avg_stack}} silently render '[Unknown token]'. Add aliases in add_builtin_tokens() or rename keys.

title: Clock public display mode: full-screen + wake-lock + ?screen= param
description: PRD phase-2 §1.3: no Fullscreen API (F11/button), no Wake Lock/NoSleep to prevent display sleep, no ?screen=clock URL routing for a clean chrome-free public display. Implement public display mode.

title: Clock: break auto-start + countdown + 'back at HH:MM'
description: PRD phase-2 §1.1: advance_level() (class-tournament-clock.php:307-358) does not detect break levels; break_until is stored (class-live-state-manager.php:278) but excluded from all heartbeat/AJAX payloads so frontend cannot render countdown or 'back at' time. Add break detection on advance + expose break_until + render.

title: Clock: sound notifications (5min/1min/level-change/break)
description: PRD phase-2 §1.1: no Web Audio usage, no audio assets, no trigger logic anywhere in clock JS. Add audio assets + sound-manager module wired to local countdown + level-change events.

title: Clock: add avg chip stack + current pot display
description: PRD phase-2 §1.1: avg chip stack and current pot are absent from live state, payload (ajax_get_clock_state:254-266) and UI. prize_pool != current pot. Add computed/stored fields, payload exposure, rendering.

title: Clock public display: add SB/BB/Ante + next-level preview
description: PRD phase-2 §1.1: public [tournament_clock] shortcode shows only the level number (class-tournament-clock-shortcode.php:160-169); no SB/BB/Ante blind values and no next-level preview are fetched or exposed in the heartbeat/AJAX payload. Add blind-schedule lookup + payload fields + template/JS rendering. (Admin timer-tab.php has blind elements but those are admin-only.)

title: PHPUnit harness + AJAX security-path tests
description: Stand up WP_UnitTestCase harness; add tests asserting nonce-missing and insufficient-capability requests are rejected for every state-changing AJAX handler (per testing rule).
close_reason: Merged in PR #23 (squash ab53913): AJAX security hardening — IDOR/DoS/import fixes, debug removal, TDWP_Ajax_Guards + 15 tests. CI green, adversarially verified SHIP.

title: EPIC: Quality - Tests, CI & Docs
description: PHPUnit harness with security-path coverage, parser edge cases, php -l + PHPCS CI, and doc reconciliation.
notes: Test harness stood up: no-DB PHPUnit 12 harness (tests/ + phpunit.xml.dist + run-tests.sh + composer.json) under wordpress-plugin/poker-tournament-import. WP stub layer (tests/stubs/wp-stubs.php with TDWP_Fake_WPDB) needs no MySQL/svn/composer; runs offline + on self-hosted runner. StatsBridgeTest: 14 tests / 26 assertions, all green. CI: .github/workflows/php-tests.yml (self-hosted macOS) + php -l lint step. CAVEAT: existing runner is repo-scoped to hkhard/HandRecorder; a runner must be registered to hkhard/tdwpimport (steps in tests/README.md). REMAINING for this bead: AJAX security-path tests (nonce/cap rejection).

title: EPIC: AJAX / Userland Security Hardening
description: Harden the ~80 admin-ajax handlers, focusing on the 12 unauthenticated (nopriv) endpoints. Admin live-control surface (class-tournament-manager-ajax.php) is already correctly guarded via verify_request().
close_reason: All AJAX/userland security hardening shipped across PRs #23-25 + ADR 0001: nopriv surface audited+hardened (IDOR/DoS/import/debug), nopriv cleanup, authenticated audit, per-IP registration limit, REST-vs-ajax decided. Follow-ups tdwp-9pi/tdwp-2mn (mobile REST) tracked separately.

title: DECISION: REST API (PRD) vs admin-ajax (current)
description: PRDs specify /wp-json/poker-tournament/v1/* REST endpoints; the plugin is admin-ajax only. The Expo controller/mobile-app needs a clean API. Decide: build REST per PRD, or ratify admin-ajax and update PRDs. Record as ADR.
notes: ADR 0001 drafted (docs/adr/0001-api-interface-ajax-vs-rest.md), status Proposed: recommend ratifying admin-ajax + deferring REST to tdwp-9pi. Awaiting user ratification before closing.
close_reason: ADR 0001 ACCEPTED (reversible): ratify admin-ajax; defer REST to tdwp-9pi (built with mobile controller). Removed backwards depends-on tdwp-9pi (decision precedes build). docs/adr/0001.

title: EPIC: PRD <-> Implementation Reconciliation
description: Phases 1-3 are largely already built. Reconcile the 4 PRDs to reality and resolve the REST-vs-admin-ajax architecture divergence before doing phase gap-work.

title: Secure ajax_unregister_screen (nopriv): add capability + fix IDOR
description: poker-tournament-import.php:3061 (registered nopriv :247-248) performs a state-changing $wpdb->update to mark a display screen offline with only a nonce check and intval(screen_id) - no capability or ownership check. An unauthenticated visitor can take any screen offline by iterating screen_id. Add current_user_can or a signed per-screen token, or restrict to authenticated screen owners.
close_reason: Merged in PR #23 (squash ab53913): AJAX security hardening — IDOR/DoS/import fixes, debug removal, TDWP_Ajax_Guards + 15 tests. CI green, adversarially verified SHIP.

title: Throttle/authorize ajax_frontend_refresh_statistics (DoS)
description: poker-tournament-import.php:2605 (nopriv) gates only on is_user_logged_in() then runs a full synchronous statistics_engine->refresh_statistics(). Any subscriber can hammer it -> CPU/DB DoS. Require a capability and/or move to wp_schedule_single_event with a throttle transient.
close_reason: Merged in PR #23 (squash ab53913): AJAX security hardening — IDOR/DoS/import fixes, debug removal, TDWP_Ajax_Guards + 15 tests. CI green, adversarially verified SHIP.

title: Harden ajax_frontend_import_tournament: capability + content sniff
description: poker-tournament-import.php:2515 allows any logged-in user (incl. subscriber) to import a .tdt and create posts; validates extension only, not content/MIME, and uses raw $_FILES handling. Add a real capability check, content sniffing via wp_check_filetype_and_ext, size cap, and wp_handle_upload.
close_reason: Merged in PR #23 (squash ab53913): AJAX security hardening — IDOR/DoS/import fixes, debug removal, TDWP_Ajax_Guards + 15 tests. CI green, adversarially verified SHIP.

title: Remove debug error_log spam (print_r of $_REQUEST/$_FILES, Beta markers)
description: ajax_frontend_import_tournament and others dump $_REQUEST/$_FILES via error_log(print_r(...)) and emit [TDWP Beta20/21] markers. Violates project rule 'no error_log debug spam in committed code' and leaks request data to logs. Route through TDWP_Debug_Logger gated by a debug flag.
close_reason: Merged in PR #23 (squash ab53913): AJAX security hardening — IDOR/DoS/import fixes, debug removal, TDWP_Ajax_Guards + 15 tests. CI green, adversarially verified SHIP.

title: Remove tdwp_ajax_test debug handler from production
description: poker-tournament-import.php:241-242 registers wp_ajax(_nopriv)_tdwp_ajax_test -> tdwp_ajax_test_handler (:3221) which only error_logs and returns 'AJAX works!'. Remove or gate behind WP_DEBUG.

title: Full AJAX handler security matrix audit (~80 handlers)

title: [HandTimer] Hand timer subsystem entirely unimplemented
description:
All five PRD §2 features (per-hand timing, hand duration analytics, hand-based breaks, slow-play warnings, average hand duration tracking) are absent. The wp_poker_hand_history table does not exist in WordPress or controller migrations. No PHP class, AJAX handler, REST endpoint, TypeScript service, or mobile-app screen implements any part of the hand timer feature.

Source audit: tdwp-ebd. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [MultiMon] Player photos not rendered on rankings display screen
description:
The PRD requires a player rankings screen with photos. The tdwp_player_photos table exists and a 'rankings' layout component is defined, but no code connects photos to the rankings display rendering (no token, no template logic, no photo retrieval in template-engine or layout-builder).

Source audit: tdwp-bro. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [MultiMon] PokerTournament.Display JS API and monitor-index not implemented
description:
The PRD specifies a JavaScript API PokerTournament.Display.register({screen_id, monitor, layout}) and a monitor field for assigning screens to physical monitors. Neither the JS API object nor a monitor-index column in poker_display_screens exists. Without this, true multi-monitor orchestration from a single control surface is impossible.

Source audit: tdwp-bro. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [MultiMon] Custom table blueprint editor missing from seating chart
description:
The PRD requires a seating chart with custom table blueprints. While a 'seating_chart' layout component type is registered, no table-shape data model, blueprint editor UI, or blueprint rendering logic exists. The seating chart component renders as a generic grid placeholder only.

Source audit: tdwp-bro. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: [Rake/Fin] Implement tax reporting export
description:
PRD §5 requires tax reporting exports. No export feature of any kind exists for financial or tax data. Need to design and implement a downloadable report (CSV or similar) covering prize winnings, rake, and other taxable amounts per player or per tournament period.

Source audit: tdwp-3jh. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

Source audit: tdwp-3jh. Feature gap (Phase 3/4).

title: Option C part (a): collapse dual player-stats store onto one canonical schema

The destructive part: merge tdwp_tournament_players (live, post-ID keyed) and poker_tournament_players (legacy, UUID keyed) into ONE canonical table so the TDWP_Stats_Bridge projection (tdwp-iwc Option A) becomes unnecessary. Requires: canonical schema design, a data-migration script (both directions), rewrite of all readers/writers across import + live + stats + dashboard + leaderboard, backward-compat during transition, and validation against real production data. NOT safe to do blindly in an autonomous loop — needs a fresh session, a written migration plan, and a staging/real-data dry run. title: Option C: consolidate live/legacy stats schema + own ROI rebuild
description: Long-term successor to the Option A bridge (tdwp-iwc, closed). Option A projects live tournaments (tdwp_tournament_players, post-ID keyed) into the legacy marts (poker_tournament_players + poker_player_roi, UUID keyed) on completion, which works but duplicates a data model. Residual architectural work: (a) collapse the dual store onto one canonical schema so projection is unnecessary; (b) make Poker_Statistics_Engine::calculate_all_statistics() OWN the poker_player_roi rebuild so every writer benefits instead of each path hand-populating ROI -- also fixes the latent no-unique-key duplication bug (poker_player_roi has only PRIMARY KEY(id), so $wpdb->replace() in process_player_roi_data/migrate_populate_roi_table never replaces, it always inserts); (c) first-class live buy-in / prize-pool (pot) data model surfaced to stats, replacing the bridge's template-lookup approximation (NB: tdwp_tournament_live_state has NO buyin_amount column although class-tdt-exporter.php:76 reads it -- latent bug; live buy-in currently lives only on the referenced template).
notes: Found during tdwp-iwc Option A verification (adversarial workflow, 2026-06-29). Exceeds the additive-listener scope of Option A.
close_reason: Parts (b)+(c) shipped in PR #27 (squash caab40d, v3.6.10): fixed poker_player_roi duplication (delete-then-insert / clear-then-rebuild), fixed undefined get_the_gmdate() fatal, added tdwp_tournament_live_state.buyin_amount column + null-safe exporter read, +RoiRebuildTest. Adversarial review caught & fixed a lossy-rebuild regression. Part (a) — destructive dual-table schema consolidation + template-aware buy-in + non-lossy rebuild ownership — split to tdwp-eil for a dedicated validated migration.

title: Add per-IP rate-limiting to nopriv ajax_register_player
description: ajax_register_player (class-player-registration.php) is correctly guarded (nonce, honeypot, sanitization, status=pending) but has no rate-limiting. A bot with a valid public nonce that defeats the honeypot could spam pending player registrations. A global throttle transient (TDWP_Ajax_Guards::is_throttled) is wrong here - it would block legitimate concurrent registrations - so implement per-IP (or per-session) limiting. LOW severity; mitigated by honeypot + pending review. Found in tdwp-vyz nopriv audit.
close_reason: Added per-IP rate-limiting (TDWP_Ajax_Guards::is_rate_limited, REMOTE_ADDR-keyed, 5/hr default) to ajax_register_player + 3 tests. v3.6.8.

title: Integrate Expo controller + mobile-app with REST API
description: Wire controller/ (Fastify) and mobile-app/ (Expo) to the new REST API; reconcile the tdwp_ SQLite controller schema with the WP canonical schema.
notes:

SUMMARY: The Expo controller (Fastify + SQLite) and mobile app (Expo/React Native) are substantially built as standalone systems — tournament CRUD, timer, blind schedules, player management, offline sync, and local notifications are all implemented and wired together. However the defining Phase 4 integration requirement — connecting this controller/mobile stack to the WordPress plugin via a versioned REST API — is entirely absent; ADR 0001 deferred it to bead tdwp-9pi which has not been worked. The controller and mobile app operate against their own tdwp_ SQLite with no bridge back to the plugin's poker_* data mart or WP post types. All add-on products (SMS/Twilio, Advanced Reporting Pack) and several PRD features (player self-service, server-push notifications, hand timer, multi-monitor API, kiosk/hotkeys) are missing.

title: Phase 3: Events & notifications (sounds, email/SMS, triggers)
description: Event trigger system, sound library + custom upload, email/SMS, priority queue, action hooks vs PRD section 2. Code: class-tournament-events.php.
notes:
AUDIT COMPLETE (2026-06-30, ultracode, adversarially verified). Phase 3: Events & Notifications (sounds, email/SMS, triggers).
Tally: partial:4 · missing:2.
P0 DEFECTS (tdwp-3lg): Event queue processor is missing — scheduled cron hook has no handler; Sound library asset files are missing from the repository.
SUMMARY: The Events and Notifications feature area is partially scaffolded but largely unimplemented. Database tables for the event queue, sound library, and notification preferences exist and are created on install (class-td3-database-schema.php). An audit-log class (class-tournament-events.php) captures tournament events and fires a WordPress action hook. However the entire dispatch layer is absent: the scheduled event-queue processor has no PHP handler, the sound asset files are missing from disk, there is no runtime playback integration, no custom-upload UI, and no email or SMS sending code. Two existing bugs — the cron hook with no handler and the seeded DB rows pointing to missing sound files — qualify as P0 defects in shipped code.

value: Live tournament stats surface in TWO legacy marts: poker_tournament_players (dashboard counts + per-player stats, joined to posts via postmeta tournament_uuid/player_uuid) AND poker_player_roi (the get_player_leaderboard/get_top_players panels read ONLY from ROI). Any writer of live results must populate BOTH. poker_player_roi has no unique key (only PRIMARY KEY id), so $wpdb->replace() never dedupes - use delete-then-insert on tournament_uuid. calculate_all_statistics() does NOT rebuild ROI. Live buy-in is NOT in _buy_in postmeta (importer-only); it lives on tdwp_tournament_templates.buy_in via tdwp_tournament_live_state.template_id (live_state has no buyin_amount column despite class-tdt-exporter.php:76 reading it - latent bug, see tdwp-ayg).
